what_it_does: This file is an orienteering ledger entry that catalogues the `foundation` terrain: a static markdown corpus of founding transcripts and synthesized doctrine, recording its location, size, read coverage, status, and its live use by another node.
observations: YAML frontmatter labels it `type: terrain-entry`, `register: descriptive`, `status: unconfirmed`, `kind: data`
The directory holds 60 markdown files (~756K) across `exchanges/`, `system/`, `models/`, and `operations/`
Only 8 of 60 files have been read; file bodies were not read, only listings
It was moved into `/home/tim/company/foundation` on 2026-06-26 from the former `~/foundation`; `move_intent: done`
`last_active: 2026-06-26` is explicitly flagged as the move/copy timestamp, not necessarily fresh authorship
It has no git remote and `secrets: false`
standouts: A static-looking archive is actually a runtime doctrine source for `model_of_tim.py`
The entry carefully distinguishes move-date mtimes from actual content edit dates
Coverage is very low relative to the claimed importance of the corpus
concerns: `status: unconfirmed` despite being a live input to a running node
Only 8/60 files read and bodies not inspected, so claims about contents are listing-level
Hardcoded consumer path `~/company/foundation/system/principles.md` may need verification after the move
`last_active` is move-date, which could mislead freshness queries
notes: A ledger record for the Company's foundation doctrine archive that flags its live use by `model_of_tim.py`, its recent move into the company tree, and the limits of current read coverage.
questions: What verification would change `status` from `unconfirmed` to `confirmed`?
Does `model_of_tim.py` still resolve the path correctly after the move from `~/foundation`?
Which other foundation files, if any, are read by live code besides `system/principles.md`?
Why is `data-store-for` set to `[[company]]` while also being `part-of: [[company]]`?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is 'the Company's memory of its own origins'; actual role is that plus a live doctrine source for `model_of_tim.py`, which is noted but not fully reflected in the `unconfirmed` status.
intention_signals: To treat the founding conversations and derived doctrine as a durable, referenceable substrate for modeling Tim and the Company, while transparently tracking where it lives, how much of it has been verified, and how it is consumed.
novelty: It documents a static markdown corpus as both an archive and a live runtime input, with explicit caveats distinguishing move timestamps from content freshness.
contribution: Provides a catalogued, caveated record of the foundation corpus and its live binding, preserving the archive's role and the limits of current verification for later fusion and querying.
summary_for_embedding: An orienteering ledger entry describing `company/foundation`, a 60-file markdown archive of founding transcripts and doctrine that is read live by `model_of_tim.py` and was moved into the company tree on 2026-06-26.
intention_for_embedding: To catalogue the foundation doctrine corpus, record its live use as a Tim-modeling input, and flag its low read coverage and move-date freshness caveats.
